decision_model.frozen_invariant: A Gate A rule that later gates must not reopen without an architecture change.
decision_model.future_decision_slot: A named later-gate choice with constraints, intentionally not decided in Gate A.
decision_model.deferred_outside_core: A contract kept out of the core repository.
decision_model.must_not_decide_yet: A choice that would imply premature product runtime behavior.

decisions.summary: Node.js 20+ and Python 3.11+ are the Gate A runtimes.
npm with package-lock.json is the JavaScript package manager.
The ordinary command surface is init, sync, status, restore.
Gate A code is limited to help/version, discovery, validation, and contract verification.
docs/architecture sources are immutable manifest-verified inputs.
schemas/ and examples/ are non-authoritative working mirrors.
Identifiers are opaque and prefix-scoped.
Relative paths are normalized, UTF-8, safe, and forward-slash based.
SHA-256 is the mandatory digest algorithm.
Tree-content digests hash canonical UTF-8 JSON for sorted complete tree content.
Material, semantic, workflow, and projection records remain separate families.
Authority descriptors require immutable registration decisions before acceptance.
Assertions and decisions are distinct immutable records.
Deterministic and model-assisted projections are separate attributable outputs.
Python hooks are schema-governed read-only stdin/stdout participants.
Adapter inspection, cursors, idempotency, reconciliation, policy, and writer partitioning are deferred outside core.
Core extension exports are narrow and documented.
Gate A contains no locks, stores, transactions, recovery, or receipts.
Source-content security is governance and test contract until later runtime phases.
Gate A closure requires invariant decisions, future slots, schemas, registries, seeded fixtures, generated descriptors, and validator parity to agree.

future_decision_slots.summary: Select the concrete identifier generator and prefix grammar validation before material identity implementation.
constraints: Identifiers must not encode path, workflow, authority, or semantic meaning.

future_decision_slots.summary: Specify locking, staging, rename, fsync, reflink, retention, and integrity-audit behavior before material storage implementation.
constraints: Material commits remain atomic and recoverable.
Hard links to writable working-tree files remain prohibited.
Recovery must not invent semantic decisions.

future_decision_slots.summary: Choose authority-source trust defaults and local versus remote semantic profiles before semantic runtime implementation.
constraints: Authority requires an immutable source-registration decision.
Model-assisted output remains proposed unless explicitly accepted.

future_decision_slots.summary: Specify archive quarantine, secret handling, sandbox, licensing, and remote-processing defaults before hardening closure.
constraints: Imported and generated content remains untrusted data.
Generated or imported code must not be executed as instructions.
Remote processing must respect configured privacy and licensing limits.
